L1.area: Dataset loading and remote data source retrieval

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where a filename extracted from a remote server's HTTP response header is used to construct a local file path without proper sanitization. An attacker controlling the server can supply a crafted filename containing path traversal sequences or absolute paths, leading to arbitrary file write on the local system, which can result in remote code execution.

L3.area: Dataset loading and remote data source retrieval
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where a filename extracted from a remote server's HTTP response header is used to construct a local file path without proper sanitization. An attacker controlling the server can supply a crafted filename containing path traversal sequences or absolute paths, leading to arbitrary file write on the local system, which can result in remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The filename from the Content-Disposition HTTP header is used to build a local file path without sanitization, allowing an attacker-controlled server to write arbitrary files via path traversal sequences.
